perturbation.original_text: Selling Parties represent to the Company and Meritage, and Selling Parties acknowledge, that: (i) they are willing and able to engage in a business that is not a Competing Business; (ii) enforcement of the restrictions set forth in this Section 1 would not be unduly burdensome to Selling Parties; (iii) the period of time provided for in this Section 1 and the territorial restrictions and other provisions and restrictions set forth herein are reasonable and necessary to protect the Company and its successors and assigns in the use and employment of the goodwill of the business conducted by Perma-Bilt Homes prior to the Effective Date; and (iv) damages cannot compensate the Company in the event of a violation of this Section 1, and that if such violation should occur, injunctive relief shall be essential for the protection of the Company and its successors and assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: Selling Parties represent to the Company and Meritage, and Selling Parties acknowledge, that: (i) they are *not* willing and able to engage in a business that is not a Competing Business; (ii) enforcement of the restrictions set forth in this Section 1 would not be unduly burdensome to Selling Parties; (iii) the period of time provided for in this Section 1 and the territorial restrictions and other provisions and restrictions set forth herein are reasonable and necessary to protect the Company and its successors and assigns in the use and employment of the goodwill of the business conducted by Perma-Bilt Homes prior to the Effective Date; and (iv) damages cannot compensate the Company in the event of a violation of this Section 1, and that if such violation should occur, injunctive relief shall be essential for the protection of the Company and its successors and assigns.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Selling Parties *are* willing and able to engage in a business that is not a Competing Business. The changed text states they are *not* willing. This creates an in-text contradiction. This contradicts the very premise of the agreement being based on their commitment to non-competition. This is contradictory to the obligations imposed in Section 1(b)(i).
perturbation.contradicted_text: Selling Parties represent to the Company and Meritage, and Selling Parties acknowledge, that: (i) they are *not* willing and able to engage in a business that is not a Competing Business; (ii) enforcement of the restrictions set forth in this Section 1 would not be unduly burdensome to Selling Parties; (iii) the period of time provided for in this Section 1 and the territorial restrictions and other provisions and restrictions set forth herein are reasonable and necessary to protect the Company and its successors and assigns in the use and employment of the goodwill of the business conducted by Perma-Bilt Homes prior to the Effective Date; and (iv) damages cannot compensate the Company in the event of a violation of this Section 1, and that if such violation should occur, injunctive relief shall be essential for the protection of the Company and its successors and assigns.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 1(i) such that the Selling Parties represent that they are *not* willing and able to engage in a non-competing business. The original text states that they *are* willing and able. This creates a direct contradiction within Section 1 and with the contradicted text. The contradicted text states that Selling Parties are not willing and able to engage in a business that is not a Competing Business, and the change to the initial text causes a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Selling Parties hereby covenant and agree that, in the event any of the provisions of this Section 1 shall be violated or breached, the Company shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the party or parties violating such covenants, without bond but upon due notice, in addition to such further or other relief as may be available at equity or law.
perturbation.changed_text: Selling Parties hereby covenant and agree that, in the event any of the provisions of this Section 1 shall be violated or breached, the Company *may not* be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the party or parties violating such covenants, without bond but upon due notice, in addition to such further or other relief as may be available at equity or law.
perturbation.explanation: The original text *guarantees* the Company's right to injunctive relief in case of a breach. The changed text casts *doubt* on this right by saying the company *may not* be entitled, which renders the enforceability of the Non-compete clause uncertain. This is contradictory to the obligations imposed in Section 1(b).
perturbation.contradicted_text: Selling Parties hereby covenant and agree that, in the event any of the provisions of this Section 1 shall be violated or breached, the Company *may not* be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the party or parties violating such covenants, without bond but upon due notice, in addition to such further or other relief as may be available at equity or law. Obtainment of such an injunction by the Company shall not be considered an election of remedies or a waiver of any right to assert any other remedies which the Company has at law or in equity. No waiver of any breach or violation hereof shall be implied from forbearance or failure by the Company to take action thereof. Zenith agrees to pay any and all reasonable costs and expenses, including attorneys' fees, incurred by the Company in enforcing this provision if it is determined that Selling Parties breached this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the company 'shall be entitled' to injunctive relief. The changed text replaces this with 'may not be entitled', directly contradicting the original guarantee. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text in this instance, but the explanation details how this change undermines the original text and now creates a contradiction by introducing uncertainty where there was previously a guarantee of injunctive relief.

perturbation.original_text: Selling Parties further agree and authorize the Company to notify others, including customers of the Company and any such future enterprise or third-party to which either Zenith or Seller may become a partner, member, owner or investor, of the terms of this Section 1 and of their obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Selling Parties further agree and authorize the Company *not* to notify others, including customers of the Company and any such future enterprise or third-party to which either Zenith or Seller may become a partner, member, owner or investor, of the terms of this Section 1 and of their obligations hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The original text authorizes the Company to inform relevant parties about the non-compete agreement. The modified text *prohibits* the Company from doing so, which undermines the purpose of the agreement and makes it difficult to enforce due to lack of awareness. This change reduces the effectiveness of Section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Selling Parties further agree and authorize the Company *not* to notify others, including customers of the Company and any such future enterprise or third-party to which either Zenith or Seller may become a partner, member, owner or investor, of the terms of this Section 1 and of their obligations hereunder. (d) Selling Parties hereby agree that the period of time in which this Section 1 is in effect shall be extended for a period equal to the duration of any breach of this Section 1(a) by Selling Parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows the Company to notify others of the terms of Section 1, while the changed text explicitly prohibits the Company from doing so. The contradicted text repeats the changed text 'Selling Parties further agree and authorize the Company *not* to notify others'. This creates a direct in-text contradiction because the original and contradicted texts hold opposing instructions for the Company which leads to structural flaw in the contract. This situation falls under 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction', where conflicting implications arise from contradictory clauses.

perturbation.original_text: Selling Parties hereby agree that the period of time in which this Section 1 is in effect shall be extended for a period equal to the duration of any breach of this Section 1(a) by Selling Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Selling Parties hereby agree that the period of time in which this Section 1 is in effect *shall not* be extended for a period equal to the duration of any breach of this Section 1(a) by Selling Parties.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, if there's a breach, the restriction period gets extended. Now, it *won't* be extended, regardless of breaches. This makes the restriction time limited, without accounting for infringements. That defeats the essence of the non compete aggreement, in the aspect that it is time-sensetive. This change impacts the duration and enforceability of Section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Selling Parties hereby agree that the period of time in which this Section 1 is in effect *shall not* be extended for a period equal to the duration of any breach of this Section 1(a) by Selling Parties. (e) Nothing contained in this Agreement shall prohibit Selling Parties or their affiliates or associates, any of their directors, officers or employees, or any of their representatives from investing in stocks, bonds, or other securities of any entity that engages in a Competing Business, provided, however, that in the case of capital stock, such securities are listed on a national securities exchange or traded in the over-the-counter market or registered under Section 12(g) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, and such investment does not exceed, in the case of any class of the capital stock of any one issuer, three percent of the issued and outstanding voting power at the time of such investment.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the restriction period would be extended in case of a breach. The changed text explicitly states that the restriction period will *not* be extended, regardless of a breach. The contradicted text repeats the statement introduced by the change, namely that the period of restriction shall not be extended, however this section also includes language regarding investment in companies that engage in a competing business. By including the changed and contradicted text in the same location, the document now presents conflicting statements about whether Section 1 may be extended or not, depending on how the section is read. The original and changed text are diametrically opposed. This constitutes a direct contradiction as it creates uncertainty regarding the enforcement of the non-compete agreement's time restrictions and thus creates ambiguity about the obligations of each party.
